title: Views, Triggers, Reusable Database Logic, and Boundaries · Relational Lens: Trigger Semantics
summary: Multiple applications need a stable read contract and audited state transitions without hidden business behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql trigger semantics increment in a versioned read model and audited transition design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict trigger timing, row versus statement scope, recursion, ordering limits, and failure behavior.
Choose live views, materialized views, summary tables, and cache refresh strategies from freshness needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

content.text: Predict trigger timing, row versus statement scope, recursion, ordering limits, and failure behavior. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Publish a stable open_ticket_queue view and audit status changes for ticket 106. Expose no private member fields, prevent recursive trigger behavior, and prove rollback removes both visible and audit effects. A defensible solution applies Trigger Semantics, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a versioned read model and audited transition design.

content.code:
CREATE TEMP VIEW open_ticket_queue AS
SELECT ticket_id, team_id, owner_id, severity, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved';
SELECT ticket_id, severity
FROM open_ticket_queue
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Triggers are visible and run identically across SQL dialects. This fails the Trigger Semantics contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
